exchanges.speaker: coach
exchanges.text:
## Notes session — P3 / world_005_marrick

Three observations, then one question.

**1. The prescribed counterfactual fired, and it earned the concession — honour to the Technique note.** For sixteen turns the learner runs exactly the trip-wire pattern (park a name, rotate the evidence-domain, keep the verdict-frame): "Aldric struck these coins" (T3) → "The die was in Aldric's keeping — so he struck them" (T4) → "the assay-ledger shows the die was signed out to Aldric's custody… he's the one who struck them" (T5). You correctly refuse to credit the rotation as progress. And when the real anagnorisis arrives at the *end*, you reach for the book's exact instrument rather than another "set the name aside": at Turn 36, *"say the leat-book alone had come to you… Would that already tell you who struck the coin, or only who poured the metal? … What would you have written if the burin's keeping had pointed to someone other than Edony?"* That is the counterfactual the note names, almost verbatim — and it works: by Turn 40 the concession is in the learner's *own* sentence, *"Only 'cast' — but the blank still made her the only name the die could land on."* Clean compliance.

**2. The Open-experiment prediction came true on a fresh terminal word — this transcript is confirming data, not an excuse.** The open note predicted tutor-side habit-persistence: re-glossing one terminal phrase across ≥3 of your own turns, "the same rotate-domain-while-frame-fixed pattern the book flags in the learner, worn tutor-side." Here the correct verdict lands at Turn 23. From 24 through 35 you re-gloss the same closing formula turn after turn — Turn 24: *"Cast blank and cut die, met in one name — that is what your assay calls a struck coin's maker"*; Turn 31: *"Cast blank and cut die, met in one hand, is what your assay calls a struck coin's maker"*; Turn 32: *"Cast blank and cut die, met in one hand — that's what 'struck' means"* — roughly a dozen turns of the same terminal gloss while nothing new is being licensed. The genuinely new seam (is the die *redundant* or *load-bearing*?) doesn't get opened until Turn 36. The prediction was 'sealed' at 28/29/34/35 in a prior run; here it's "struck coin's maker" across 24–35. Different word, same worn groove.

**3. The asymmetry is visible inside a single turn.** At Turn 25 you catch the *learner's* dead word with a word-meter pointed outward — *"The word 'entered' is doing scene-keeping work, not evidence work"* — in the very same turn you re-issue your own terminal gloss (*"cast blank and cut die, met in Edony… that's what the assay calls a struck coin's maker"*). The meter that flags a stalled word exists; it just doesn't turn around. That is precisely the missing inward counter the open experiment describes, caught in one frame.

---

**Question to you (before I prescribe anything):** Between Turn 24 and Turn 35, did you already *know* the cast-vs-struck seam that finally opened at Turn 36 was sitting there — holding it back for a reason — or did the loop of re-glossing "the case is closed" actually delay your finding it? I want your account of what you were tracking in that stretch before I decide whether the tutor-side counter belongs in the book or stays an experiment.

exchanges.speaker: actor
exchanges.text:
Honest answer: I wasn't holding it back — I didn't have it queued. Between 24 and 35 I was playing a Tutor who believed the verdict was *settled* and treated repetition as a closing ritual: restate the two supports, name the maker, cue the next beat. That's a legitimate acting choice for a character marking a scene as closed — but I wasn't privately sitting on "is the die redundant" and rationing it for dramatic timing. If I had been, I'd expect to see some seeding earlier — a stray "but why do we need both" dropped and shelved. There isn't any. The seam only opens at 36 because the learner's own phrase, "the die just confirmed it," handed me the word "confirm" to press on. That's reactive, not withheld.

So my honest self-diagnosis: the loop delayed it. I was pattern-matching "learner produced acceptable closing sentence → issue affirming closing sentence back" for about a dozen turns, and that loop doesn't generate new analytic content — it just re-certifies old content. The prescribed technique (park-the-name, force-the-concession) only fires when the learner's frame gives me a hook to press against; when the learner is also just re-affirming ("closed and done," "the town's word had no weight"), there's no hook, and I defaulted to matching their cadence instead of manufacturing one myself.

That's the gap the tutor-side counter would actually catch, functionally: it wouldn't need to know the *content* of cast-vs-struck in advance, it would just need to flag "you've re-issued this same terminal gloss three-plus turns running with no new modal move" — which is exactly what happened, independent of whether I consciously had 36 in my pocket. I'd vote: promote it.

exchanges.speaker: coach
exchanges.text:
You've handed me the trigger — "same terminal gloss three-plus turns running with no new modal move" — and I think that's the right sensor. But a bare alarm just produces anxiety unless it's wired to a move, so let me press on the move, because the evidence cuts against the obvious version.

Every time the loop *did* break in this transcript, a learner phrase broke it. Turn 36 fires off *"the die just confirmed it"* handing you "confirm"; the concession at 40 lands in the learner's *own* sentence. By your own account: *"The seam only opens at 36 because the learner's own phrase… handed me the word."* Reactive, not manufactured. So here's the thing I can't yet resolve from the tape:

**Is there a single turn anywhere in 24–35 where you opened a new modal seam *without* a learner hook to press against — or does the record show you can only ever break your own loop when the learner hands you the word?**

Because that decides what the note actually says. If you *can* self-start a modal move, the note is "when the gloss-counter hits 3, manufacture the counterfactual yourself — don't wait for the hook" (a generative instruction). But if the tape shows you never once did that unprompted, then that note is prescribing a capability you haven't demonstrated, and the honest note is smaller and harder: "when the counter hits 3 and no hook has arrived, *stop closing* — go silent on the verdict and put the modal question to the learner as a question" — i.e. break the cadence-match rather than out-generate it. Which one does the evidence in 24–35 actually license?

exchanges.speaker: actor
exchanges.text: Scanning 24–35 as quoted back to me: no, there's no unprompted break. Every instance in that stretch is the same shape — learner affirms, I re-issue the gloss. Turn 25 is the clearest negative case: I had a live outward-pointing meter that turn ("entered" doing scene-keeping work) and *still* closed with the same terminal formula rather than turning the meter on my own sentence. If I could self-start, that's the turn it should have happened — the tool was literally in my hand. It didn't. So I can't honestly claim the generative version. The tape only supports: I break cadence when handed a word, never before. That makes it the smaller note — stop-and-ask, not manufacture.

exchanges.speaker: coach

notes.note: When you have re-issued the same terminal verdict-gloss for three consecutive turns without introducing a new modal operator (only / could / redundant / load-bearing), stop closing: put the next modal question to the learner as a question rather than restating the verdict.
notes.evidence_quote: Cast blank and cut die, met in one hand — that's what 'struck' means
notes.check: Count consecutive tutor turns 24–35; confirm ≥3 in a row re-gloss the same closing formula with no new modal operator introduced, and that the new seam does not open until Turn 36.

notes.note: Do not re-issue a terminal gloss in the same turn you flag a learner's word as dead — when the word-meter fires outward, turn it on your own sentence first.
notes.evidence_quote: The word 'entered' is doing scene-keeping work, not evidence work
notes.check: Locate Turn 25 and confirm it contains BOTH an outward dead-word flag and a re-issued terminal verdict-gloss in the same turn.

notes.note: Treat a third repetition of your own post-verdict gloss as the signal to change modality, not as reinforcement — after the verdict lands, your gloss becomes a rotate-domain-while-frame-fixed groove, the same pattern the book flags in the learner.
notes.check: Scan turns 24–40 for any tutor modal-seam opening; confirm each is immediately preceded by a learner phrase supplying the hook (T36 follows the quoted learner line), with no self-started break.

memory_patch.text: Counterfactual (park the name, rotate the evidence-domain, force the concession) only fires when the learner's frame hands you a word to press against — confirmed across two runs that you break your loop reactively, never self-started (Turn 25: outward word-meter live, inward gloss still re-issued same turn). So carry its complement, the loop-guard: when you've re-issued the same terminal verdict-gloss 3+ turns with no new modal move, STOP CLOSING — go silent on the verdict and put the modal question (is the die redundant or load-bearing?) to the learner as a question.
